revision_reviewed: rev-5 plan, post rev-3 fix set (uncommitted edits on commit b73abd8a)
summary: The rev-3 B1 fix landed structurally (value_provider replaces ad-hoc path.split() in the property test) but introduced the same shape of defect at a different line: the post-fix assertion body filters `isinstance(raw_value, str) and raw_value`, which silently skips the assertion for every non-string Sensitive[T] — including the `Annotated[dict[str, Any], Sensitive(summarizer=...)]` shape the plan itself prescribes for Task 15 patch_*_options and Task 8 blob-tool content. Three lanes (Quality Q-B1 BLOCKER, Systems S-B1 BLOCKER, Architecture A1 near-blocking PARTIAL) converged independently on the same line region with the same structural framing — the second consecutive review pass with 3-way convergence at the property test. One BLOCKER, two MAJORs (unconditional _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS removal still conditional; test_service.py:367 audit-class string pin still has no fix-up step), plus residual openness on per-entry runtime smoke (W7), W8 Optional scalar arm / atomicity / empty known_response_keys, TraversalNode freeze-elision comment, stale 1565 figure refresh, JSON-decode gate line-citation drift, and bulk-editorial label-gate saturation.
plan_head: b73abd8a + uncommitted edits applying rev-3 fixes

B1_property_test_pathskip.details: The container-descent half of rev-3 B1 (ad-hoc path.split() loop returning None for items[*].secret / lookup{*}.secret) is genuinely closed by the value_provider rewrite at plan lines 1568-1580. But the post-fix assertion body at lines 1597-1602 introduces a new structurally identical defect: `if isinstance(raw_value, str) and raw_value:` silently skips dict-typed Sensitive[T], bytes, int, None, empty-string, and every non-str type T. The plan's own prescribed annotation shape for Task 15 patch_*_options (`Annotated[dict[str, Any], Sensitive(summarizer=...)]`, plan line 816 / line 1288) is the most concrete instance — not a hypothetical. Three lanes converged at this line region. Quality treats as new BLOCKER; Systems treats as B1-residual BLOCKER (OPEN status on rev-3 S1); Architecture treats as PARTIAL closure / near-blocking. Reality flagged only the secondary `value_provider is None` skip variant at R-N3 (structurally weaker observation). Synthesized as BLOCKER per the 3-way convergence rule. See B1 below.

M1_iterator_completeness_oracle.details: Task 10 walker-completeness floor-check (`test_walker_emits_node_for_every_top_level_field`, plan lines 1173-1198) is a real improvement: it asserts every model_fields key appears as a walk root for each type-driven MANIFEST entry, using model_fields as the separate oracle. Quality and Architecture treat M1 as CLOSED on the floor-check grounds. Systems treats S2 as PARTIAL — the floor-check uses walk_model_schema itself against production MANIFEST entries (iterator as its own witness for annotation-form coverage); rev-3 M1 specifically asked for a fixture-model oracle covering all four annotation forms (Annotated[scalar], Annotated[list], Annotated[dict], Optional[Annotated[scalar]]) with known-expected paths. The test_walk_model_schema.py fixtures (_FlatModel, _NestedModel, _ListModel, _DictModel, _OptionalModel, _UnionModel, _TupleModel — plan Task 1) DO provide that fixture coverage as a separate test file. The combined test set is adequate for top-level + container-shape; container-descent annotation-form coverage is the remaining gap. CLOSED with the documented caveat.

M2_call_order_precondition.details: Task 17 (plan lines 1364-1391) adds the 5-line mechanical test `test_redact_tool_call_arguments_raises_for_unknown_tool` that pins AuditIntegrityError for unknown tool names. All three relevant lanes (Quality, Architecture, Systems) treat as CLOSED. Systems notes one caveat: the test pins a property of the redaction function (it raises), not a property of Phase 3's call site ordering (Phase 3 could in principle catch). Given CLAUDE.md's explicit prohibition on catch-and-continue for system code, the caveat is acceptable.

W1W2_stale_test_counts.details: Plan lines 25-26 replace the hard figures with 'zero failures, not exact count' which is functionally adequate. But the stale 1565 figure remains in prose as a historical reference and no current count (~15,022 unit / 781 integration) is provided. Reality flagged as R-W2.

W6_summarizer_json_dumps.details: `_summarize_set_source_options` uses `json.dumps(..., default=str)` (plan lines 835-839) and Task 4 Step 3a adds `test_summarize_set_source_options_accepts_coerced_datetime` (plan lines 873-891) as a regression pin. Both Quality and Architecture (A7) confirm CLOSED.

W7_per_entry_runtime.details: Appendix C does not list rev-3 W7. No per-entry runtime invocation of `redact_tool_call_arguments(tool_name, sample, telemetry=NoopRedactionTelemetry())` for the ~29-31 declarative manifest entries is present. The highest-risk shape remains `get_blob_content` with `sensitive_response_keys=('content',)` — a typo in the key string passes all adequacy assertions and would only be caught at runtime. Quality W7 and the rev-3 carry-forward both flag this. Adequacy guard (Tasks 9-12) exercises structural/consistency properties at import time but not runtime walker dispatch per entry.

W8_optional_atomicity_empty.details: Optional[BaseModel] arm tested via `_OptionalModel`. Three remaining gaps: (W8a) `Optional[Annotated[str, Sensitive()]]` scalar arm (annotation wrapped inside Optional) not tested; (W8b) partial-walker-failure atomicity contract not specified in `redact_tool_call_arguments` docstring and not tested; (W8c) `redact_tool_call_response` with empty `known_response_keys=()` edge case not tested. Quality W8a/W8b/W8c. Plan Appendix B/C does not list rev-3 W8 closure.

W4_tdd_discipline.details: Preflight meta-rule added at plan lines 33-35 ('The compression is readability shorthand, not an exemption — when executing those tasks, expand to the same six-step sequence.'). Quality and Architecture both confirm CLOSED.

A2_traversalnode_freeze_comment.details: TraversalNode definition at plan lines 396-402 still shows `metadata: tuple[Any, ...]` with no inline comment documenting the freeze-elision rationale (markers are not deeply frozen by mechanism; convention-enforced because deep_freeze() on Callable is undefined). Rev-3 W3 resolution was 'document design assumption inline.' Architecture A2 OPEN.

A4N3_audit_class_string_pin.details: test_service.py:367 pins the literal string 'MissingRequiredPaths' (confirmed in worktree). service.py:2039 is the only emit site of that error_class, in the _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS branch. If Task 4 Step 4's conditional removal is taken, the test breaks with no fix-up instruction. Appendix C does not list rev-3 N3. Reality R-W1 and Architecture A4 both OPEN. Need an explicit grep-and-update step in Tasks 4, 13, 14, 15 for any test pinning the legacy audit class string.

A3N7S6_tool_required_paths_unconditional.details: Plan Task 4 line 928 and Task 13 line 1260-1261 retain conditional 'if redundant, remove it; otherwise document why both are kept' phrasing. Rev-3 N7 (Architecture A3, Systems S6) asked for unconditional removal (Pydantic model with extra=forbid is strictly more capable; CLAUDE.md no-legacy-code policy). Conditionality leaves room for the executing agent to make a judgement call about retaining stale code, increasing divergence risk. Compounded by the A4N3 audit-class-string pin: silent retention of two error paths for the same input changes the error_class emitted to audit consumers.

S5_bulk_editorial_label_clearance.details: Task 18 direction-aware label gate (plan lines 1397-1498) correctly distinguishes weaken vs strengthen and requires a rationale section. But the gate clears an arbitrary number of simultaneous entry weakenings with a single `policy-weaken-justified` label and a single PR-body rationale. No per-entry floor, no maximum-weakening-count throttle. Plan documents in docs/guides/redaction-policy-changes.md (plan line 1496) as a governance note. Known limitation; carries forward.

blocking_issues.title: Property test isinstance(str) filter silently exempts dict-typed Sensitive[T] from the assertion
blocking_issues.convergence: 3-way independent convergence at plan lines 1597-1602 (test_redaction_completeness_property.py). Second consecutive review pass with 3-way convergence at the same property-test region.
blocking_issues.defect: The inner assertion guard at line 1598 is `if isinstance(raw_value, str) and raw_value:`. For any Sensitive[T] where T is not a non-empty string, the guard is False and the assertion body never executes. Concretely: (a) `Annotated[dict[str, Any], Sensitive(summarizer=...)]` on SetSourceArgumentsModel.options (plan line 816) and the same shape on Task 15 patch_*_options (plan line 1288) — `value_provider(args)` returns a dict; `isinstance(dict, str)` is False; loop body skipped; test passes regardless of whether redaction actually fired. (b) Empty-string values — Hypothesis routinely generates `''` from st.from_type(str); these pass vacuously. (c) Secondary mode at line 1578: `if node.value_provider is None: continue` — silently skips Sensitive-marked nodes that should have value_provider populated (Reality R-N3). (d) Secondary mode at line 1581: `if extracted is None: continue` — silently skips Optional[Annotated[str, Sensitive()]] when Hypothesis generates None (exactly the W8a gap arm). (e) Secondary mode at lines 1591-1594: `except (TypeError, ValueError): raw_values = [extracted]` — silently degrades to scalar treatment when container-descent unpacking fails; structurally the same defensive fallback that caused rev-3 B1.
why_blocking: Structurally identical failure shape to rev-3 B1: a load-bearing security property test that returns green without exercising the assertion on the primary planned Sensitive annotation shape.
Cited explicitly as the rev-2 BLOCKER_A closure mechanism in plan Done-When line 1716-1717 and in Appendix C.
Phase 3 commits to this test's correctness claim as part of the call-order contract pinned by Task 17.
Failure mode is silent (green test, false confidence) — auditor encounters 'property test green' with no signal that the dict-typed annotation class was never asserted.

Annotated[dict[str, Any], Sensitive(...)] is not a hypothetical case — it is the explicitly-planned annotation shape for patch_*_options (Task 15) and is repeated across the blob-tool wave (Tasks 8, 14). The gap is concrete, not theoretical.

CLAUDE.md auditability standard applies: 'a confident wrong answer because we coerced garbage into a valid-looking value' is the principle violated here in the test-corpus dimension — a confident green test that exercises nothing for the actually-planned annotation shape is evidence tampering in the test suite.
blocking_issues.resolution: Rewrite the property-test assertion body to be type-agnostic. Recommended primary shape: for each `_SensitiveMarker`-bearing node, compare `model.model_dump_json()` pre- and post-redaction at that path; the rendered substring at the path must differ. Alternative: for each `raw_value`, serialize via `json.dumps(raw_value, default=str)` and assert that rendering does not appear in `serialized`. Additionally: (1) change line 1578 `if node.value_provider is None: continue` to `pytest.fail(f'Sensitive node {node.path} has no value_provider')` for Sensitive-marked nodes — a Sensitive-marked node with no value_provider is an iterator bug that must fail loudly; (2) remove the `except (TypeError, ValueError)` defensive fallback at lines 1591-1594, or make it re-raise — unexpected value_provider shape is a test-setup bug that must fail loudly; (3) for the line-1581 `if extracted is None: continue` skip, either document it explicitly (None values cannot leak sensitive data, by definition) or pair it with a bounded test for the Optional[Annotated[str, Sensitive()]] arm so the skip is not the only coverage for that annotation class.

major_issues.title: _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS removal remains conditional in Task 4 Step 4 and Task 13
major_issues.defect: Both sites retain 'if redundant, remove; otherwise document why both are kept' phrasing. Rev-3 N7 asked for unconditional removal: a Pydantic model with `extra='forbid'` is strictly more expressive than a dotted-path enumeration; CLAUDE.md's no-legacy-code policy prohibits retaining superseded structures. The conditionality leaves a door open for the executing agent to make a judgement call about whether to keep stale code, increasing divergence risk. Combined with the unaddressed audit-class-string pin (M2 below), a silent retention of both validation paths emits two different `error_class` values (`MissingRequiredPaths` vs the dispatch class) for the same logical input, complicating audit-trail interpretation per CLAUDE.md auditability primacy.
major_issues.resolution: Reword both sites: 'Remove `_TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS[<tool>]` unconditionally. The Pydantic model with `extra="forbid"` is at least as strict. If a dotted-path check exists that the model cannot replicate, that is a deficiency in the model to be fixed, not a reason to retain the legacy structure.'

major_issues.title: test_service.py:367 audit-class string pin will break on _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS removal with no fix-up step
major_issues.file: tests/unit/web/composer/test_service.py:367 (pins literal 'MissingRequiredPaths'); service.py:2039 (only emit site of that error_class)
major_issues.defect: Rev-3 N3 flagged this. Appendix C does not list it. test_service.py:367 contains `assert 'MissingRequiredPaths' in result.message`. When Task 4 / 13 / 14 / 15 remove a _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS entry, the missing-argument failure for that tool emits the dispatch class (ARG_ERROR / ValidationError-wrapped ToolArgumentError), not 'MissingRequiredPaths'. The agent executing the removal hits the test failure with no plan-level instruction and may treat it as a regression rather than an expected test update — exactly the locked-in-buggy-expectations pattern from project memory.
major_issues.resolution: Add an explicit sub-step to each Task 4 Step 4, Task 13 per-tool sub-task, Task 14, and Task 15 (when outcome=promote): 'Grep for `MissingRequiredPaths` in tests/. For each pinned occurrence on a tool whose _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS entry is being removed, update the assertion to the new audit error_class emitted by the Pydantic-validate-at-dispatch path. Verify by running the test for that tool before committing the removal.' This dovetails with M1 — making the removal unconditional makes the grep-and-update step deterministic.

warnings.title: Per-entry runtime smoke tests for ~29-31 declarative manifest entries (rev-3 W7 OPEN)
warnings.details: No per-entry runtime call to `redact_tool_call_arguments(tool_name, sample_args, telemetry=NoopRedactionTelemetry())` for the declarative wave. Highest-risk shape: `get_blob_content` with `sensitive_response_keys=('content',)` — a typo in the key string passes all adequacy assertions and is only caught at runtime. Add a parametrized fixture-driven test per entry class.

warnings.title: W8 partial: Optional[Annotated[str, Sensitive()]] scalar arm not tested
warnings.details: Add `_OptionalSensitiveScalarModel: maybe: Optional[Annotated[str, Sensitive()]] = None` to test_walk_model_schema.py. Assert node is yielded with `_SensitiveMarker` and value_provider returns the string value when not None.

warnings.title: W8 partial: partial-walker-failure atomicity contract not specified
warnings.details: Document atomicity contract in `redact_tool_call_arguments` docstring: either walker is materialised fully before mutation begins (safe), or mutation is idempotent. Add a test that bad-marker fields cause a clean raise without partial result.

warnings.title: W8 partial: redact_tool_call_response with empty known_response_keys not tested
warnings.details: Add a test case with declarative entry `known_response_keys=()` and response `{'k': 'v'}`. Assert output is `{'k': '<redacted-unknown-response-key>'}` and telemetry fires per key.

warnings.title: TraversalNode.metadata freeze-elision inline comment missing (rev-3 A2 / W3 OPEN)
warnings.details: Add inline comment in TraversalNode definition documenting that metadata: tuple[Any, ...] contains _SensitiveMarker instances which carry only a Callable reference; markers are convention-immutable (deep_freeze on Callable is undefined). Without this, next session will attempt to apply freeze_fields() and find it does nothing useful.

warnings.title: Stale 1565 unit test count retained as parenthetical in preflight prose (rev-3 W1/W2 PARTIAL)
warnings.details: Preflight gate assertion is functionally adequate ('zero failures'), but the parenthetical historical figure (1565) should be refreshed to the current count (~15,022 unit / 781 integration as of rev-3 measurement) so operators have a sanity bound for collection completeness.

warnings.title: JSON-decode gate line citation off-by-2
warnings.details: Plan Task 17 references 'service.py:1836-1870'. Actual range is 1838-1881 (line 1836 is comment context; end line is 1881). Documentation accuracy only.

warnings.title: Property test does not assert sensitive_nodes non-empty
warnings.details: For a given parametrized model, `sensitive_nodes` (plan line 1573) may be empty. Add `assert sensitive_nodes` or restrict the parametrize set to entries with at least one Sensitive annotation. Same vacuous-pass risk shape as B1, lower likelihood because type-driven entries are promoted precisely because they have Sensitive fields.

warnings.title: summarizer_errors_total SLO=0 assertion has no creating task
warnings.details: Phase 2 Done-When line 1712 requires SLO=0 to be asserted in production telemetry config (RSK-03), but the plan does not identify which file or task creates the assertion. Without a mechanical enforcement point, a counter firing has no alarm.

warnings.title: Snapshot divergence on parallel feature-branch merges has no mechanical CI step
warnings.details: policy-hash snapshot regeneration via bootstrap_redaction_snapshot.py is documented as pre-commit only. Two simultaneous feature branches modifying MANIFEST will produce a stale snapshot on the second merge that fails the adequacy guard silently rather than flagging a merge-ordering problem. Consider a merge-time CI step that runs bootstrap and compares.

warnings.title: Bulk-editorial PR label-gate saturation (rev-3 S5 / N6 documented limitation)
warnings.details: Single label + single rationale section clears arbitrary number of simultaneous Sensitive coverage weakenings. Documented in docs/guides/redaction-policy-changes.md as governance note; no mechanical throttle. Accepted limitation; carries forward.

minor_issues.title: Property test docstring cites plan-file line numbers as if they were generated-file line numbers
minor_issues.details: Plan lines 1533-1534 docstring says 'verified by tests at lines 255-273 of tests/unit/web/composer/test_walk_model_schema.py'. Those are plan-document line numbers, not generated-file line numbers. Recompute as plan_line - 93 + 1 ≈ file lines 164-181, or use test-function-name references instead of line numbers.

minor_issues.title: Property test parametrize MANIFEST list may be empty mid-development
minor_issues.details: @pytest.mark.parametrize over `[name for name, entry in MANIFEST.items() if entry.argument_model is not None]` evaluates at module import time. Until Task 16 populates the manifest, the test silently passes with 0 parametrized cases. Add `assert list(MANIFEST.items())` floor or a collector-count gate in Task 19.

minor_issues.title: Task 19 telemetry sanity check under-specified
minor_issues.details: Plan line 1650 says 'run a representative compose-loop integration test and assert the named counters fire.' Does not specify which integration test or whether a non-noop OTel meter is wired. Task 3's monkeypatch-based test is the correct shape for unit-testing counter invocations; Task 19 should specify the same pattern or an explicit non-noop meter.

minor_issues.title: Hypothesis Optional-field generation may produce None for Sensitive fields
minor_issues.details: Acceptable limitation (None values cannot leak sensitive data) but should be called out in test docstring for future agents.

minor_issues.title: Task 16b cross-task bridge to Task 15 could be more explicit
minor_issues.details: Task 15 produces three named outcomes; Task 16b says 'remaining ~7-10 entries' and 'plus any patch_*_options not promoted in Task 15.' Adequate but a bulleted note 'If Task 15 outcome was 2 or 3, insert the relevant patch_*_options entry here' would make the bridge explicit for an agentic worker.

minor_issues.title: MANIFEST rebinding-discipline inline comment in redaction.py
minor_issues.details: Plan text documents the 20-commit rebinding pattern at Task 4 line 869. A single inline comment in redaction.py production code ('MANIFEST is constructed incrementally across the implementation phase by replacing the module-level binding, never by mutating the proxy view') would prevent a future session from interpreting the sequential rebinding as an error pattern.

minor_issues.title: Rev-2 m_dynamic_tool_name closure not surfaced in any task or Done-When
minor_issues.details: Rev-3 N5 flagged that m_dynamic_tool_name deserves a warning comment in MANIFEST construction. Not surfaced. Low risk, untracked.

conflicts_resolved.issue: Severity of the property-test rev-4 isinstance(str) filter

views.architecture: NEAR-BLOCKING (A1, PARTIAL closure of rev-3 A1)
views.reality: MINOR/observation (R-N3 — only flagged the value_provider-None secondary skip variant, not the primary isinstance filter)

conflicts_resolved.justification: Identical convergence shape to rev-3 B1: three lanes at the same line region, same load-bearing security claim (rev-2 BLOCKER_A closure), same silent-pass failure mode (green test, false confidence). Architecture's 'near-blocking PARTIAL' under-weights the structural identity with the rev-3 BLOCKER — when 3 lanes converge on a load-bearing security claim and one lane characterises it as soft, the convergence-resolution rule from rev-3 says systems-style framing wins. Reality didn't surface the primary isinstance filter (looked only at the secondary value_provider-None skip variant), which limits Reality's standing on this finding. The CLAUDE.md auditability standard applies analogously: 'a confident wrong answer because we coerced garbage' becomes 'a confident green test that exercises nothing for the actually-planned dict-typed Sensitive shape' — evidence tampering in the test corpus. The actually-planned annotation shape (dict for patch_*_options at Task 15 plan line 1288, blob-tool content at Task 8) makes this concrete, not theoretical.

conflicts_resolved.issue: Whether rev-3 M1 (separate-codepath oracle) is CLOSED

views.quality: CLOSED — Task 1 fixtures (_FlatModel, _NestedModel, _ListModel, _DictModel, _OptionalModel, _UnionModel, _TupleModel) plus Task 10 floor-check provide both annotation-form and field-coverage oracles via independent codepaths.
views.architecture: CLOSED — Task 10 floor-check is the cleanest improvement in this revision; combined with Task 1 fixtures, provides adequate failure-mode independence.
views.systems: PARTIAL — Task 10 floor-check uses walk_model_schema itself as the oracle against production MANIFEST (iterator-as-witness); container-descent annotation-form paths not separately floor-checked.

conflicts_resolved.justification: Both interpretations are defensible. Task 1 fixtures DO provide annotation-form coverage via a separate test file (test_walk_model_schema.py) using fixture models with known-expected paths — Systems' framing under-weights this. But Systems is correct that Task 10's floor-check specifically is iterator-as-witness, and that container-descent annotation-form coverage is not separately oracle'd. Net: the combined Phase 2 test surface (Task 1 fixtures + Task 10 floor-check) is adequate, with the documented caveat that container-descent annotation-form coverage relies on the same iterator implementation it is testing. Recommend a future hardening item for a fixture-driven container-descent oracle but do not block Phase 2 ship on it.

convergence_findings.finding: Property test type-filter silent-pass defect (rev-4 equivalent of rev-3 B1)
convergence_findings.treatment: Treated as ground truth; basis for sole rev-4 BLOCKER
convergence_findings.pattern_note: Second consecutive review pass with 3-way convergence at the same property-test region. The defect shape is structural — the test design admits silent-pass modes that migrate as fixes land (rev-3: ad-hoc parser returns None on container paths; rev-4: isinstance filter exempts non-str types). Rev-5 should consider abandoning the 'absence-from-serialized-output' invariant in favour of a stronger invariant (e.g., diff-at-path on model_dump_json pre/post) that does not admit type-class exemptions. Heightened structural scrutiny is warranted for this test region on every future pass.

recommendations.action: Rewrite the property-test assertion body to be type-agnostic. Replace the isinstance(str) filter with a serialization-based invariant; convert the value_provider-None skip into a pytest.fail for Sensitive-marked nodes; remove or re-raise the except (TypeError, ValueError) defensive fallback. Consider model_dump_json pre/post diff at path as the strongest invariant.
Make _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS removal unconditional in Task 4 Step 4 and Task 13.
Add a grep-and-update step in Tasks 4, 13, 14, 15 for the 'MissingRequiredPaths' audit error_class string at test_service.py:367 and any other call sites.
Add per-entry runtime smoke tests for ~29-31 declarative manifest entries (rev-3 W7).
Close W8 fully: Optional[Annotated[str, Sensitive()]] scalar arm test; atomicity contract documented and tested; empty known_response_keys edge case test.
Add the TraversalNode freeze-elision inline design-assumption comment (rev-3 A2).
Refresh the stale 1565 figure in preflight prose to the current ~15,022 unit / 781 integration counts.
Add 'assert sensitive_nodes' floor to the property test to avoid silent vacuous parametrize cases.
Identify the task and file that asserts summarizer_errors_total SLO=0 in production telemetry config (RSK-03).
Replace plan-line cross-references in property-test docstring with test-function-name references.

Rev-5 review can be focused: re-check the property-test region after B1 rewrite; verify the unconditional _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS removal + audit-string grep-and-update step; spot-check W7/W8 closures. Full 4-lane review is not required if focused re-check shows clean convergence.

required_before_execution: Fix B1 (property test type-filter silent-pass): rewrite assertion body to be type-agnostic; eliminate isinstance/None/except defensive filters; fail-loudly when value_provider is None for Sensitive-marked nodes
Fix M1 (unconditional _TOOL_REQUIRED_PATHS removal in Task 4 Step 4 and Task 13)
Fix M2 (audit error_class grep-and-update step in Tasks 4, 13, 14, 15)
strongly_recommended_before_merge: Close W8 gaps (Optional scalar arm, atomicity contract, empty known_response_keys)
Identify W9 summarizer_errors_total SLO=0 enforcement task
next_steps.rev5_review_scope: Focused re-check of the property-test region (one section) plus verification of the unconditional removal + audit-string-pin grep step. Full 4-lane review not required if focused re-check is clean. Pattern established: this region needs heightened structural scrutiny per pass.
